summary: Frontend-only verification of iter-151.28 hinge-axis-raised fix (drawer chest + parametric box). ALL FIVE review-request items pass. Chest: hinged lid Z bbox is now 5.4 mm (= 2·knuckleR, previously 3 mm = hingeLidThickness only) — knuckle now clearly protrudes above the lid top. Frame Z bbox is now 102.4 mm at hingedtop-only vs prior iter-151.23 baseline 101.2 mm — a +1.2 mm delta = knuckleR − hingeLidThickness/2 (2.7 − 1.5). Box: hinged lid Z bbox is now 5.2 mm (= 2·knuckleR), box body Z bbox is 33.2 mm = bodyH + 2·knuckleR (28 + 5.2) — matches spec `bodyH + knuckleR` for axis + knuckleR for top of knuckle. Regressions clean: hinged OFF restores exact pre-fix chest baseline (Frame 80×60×96, Top cap 86×66×4); Box lidMode=none restores Box body 60×40×30 (bodyH = H when lidMode='none'). Kickstand still functional (Frame 80×66.914×104.864 at default 100°, delta from hinged-only 80×65.4×102.4 is +Y 1.514 +Z 2.464 — stop bar tilts correctly). Add-to-workspace works for both generators; workspace shows knuckle bumps as intended piano-hinge detail with no black gaps or broken geometry. Both generators produce manifold parts with the axle-hole carve intact.

issues: 'What's New' popup (bottom-right) continues to overlap the footer's Add-to-workspace / ZIP bundle buttons on both dialogs — same overlay flagged in iter-150.6 / 151.22 / 151.23. Force-clicks bypass but a real user might misclick. MEDIUM priority. Consider gating the changelog popup so it doesn't appear over these dialogs (or at least dismissing it automatically once a param dialog opens).

critical_code_review_comments: drawerChestGenerator.js line ~336: the frame-side rib still uses `knuckleR*2` for the Z-extent (`new THREE.BoxGeometry(kLen, knuckleR + 0.5, knuckleR * 2)`) and translates to `knuckleZ - knuckleR` — that puts the rib centre at `frameTopZ + knuckleR - knuckleR = frameTopZ`, so the rib spans world Z [frameTopZ − knuckleR, frameTopZ + knuckleR]. Half of the rib therefore sits BELOW the frame top plane (i.e. INSIDE the top compartment cavity). That may be fine because the frame top slab is a solid roundedSlab (no hollowed compartment above), but if a future refactor ever hollows the topmost compartment (e.g. the topHingedBox interior) that rib will intrude into it. LOW priority — worth a comment.

boxGenerator.js line ~393: same pattern on the box side (rib Z = knuckleR*2 centred at knuckleZ − knuckleR = bodyH). Half the rib goes below the seam and will land INSIDE the box cavity. Because bodyH minus wall/floor is the interior, this creates a small bump inside the back-top-inside corner. Not visible externally, but may affect the fit of things placed inside the box. Recommend clamping rib height to Math.max(knuckleR+0.5, wall) and translating so the rib top is at knuckleZ+knuckleR while its bottom sits flush with (bodyH − wall) — same pattern used on the lid side already.

drawerChestGenerator.js: `knuckleR = Math.max(2.6, hingeLidThickness * 0.9)` (line 200) can therefore be LARGER than hingeLidThickness for thin lids (< 2.9 mm). Now with the raised-axis fix, the lid's Z extent is `max(hingeLidThickness, 2*knuckleR)` = 2*knuckleR whenever knuckleR > hingeLidThickness/2. This is intentional per the fix — but the resulting lid Z (5.4 mm at default hingeLidThickness=3, knuckleR=2.7) is nearly 2× the slab thickness. Users may be surprised that 'lid thickness 3 mm' produces a 5.4 mm-tall part. Consider surfacing an info-hint in the DrawerChestDialog when hinges are ON that reads 'Hinged lid Z = lid + knuckle bump ≈ {2·knuckleR} mm'. NICE-TO-HAVE.

Both generators do a full manifold rebuild on every param toggle (no incremental caching). On a slower machine each hingedtop toggle takes ~1.5-2 s. Adequate for now, but if further parametric controls are added to the same panel, consider memoising the frame/lid subassemblies separately.

The knuckle Y placement (`knuckleY = -D/2 - knuckleR` on chest, `+D/2 + knuckleR` on box) means the knuckle sticks fully OUTSIDE the back wall. Combined with the raised knuckleZ, the exposed axle pin now sits at world coords (-D/2 - knuckleR, frameTopZ + knuckleR). The `knuckleR + 0.5` rib gives ~0.5 mm of overlap with the back wall — thin. If someone prints the frame in ABS/PLA at a high infill and someone yanks the lid, the rib could shear at that thin bond. LOW priority; a rib depth of ~2 mm would be more robust. Consider `Math.max(knuckleR + 0.5, 2.0)`.
test_credentials: Not required — used guest bypass via ?from=test URL param. No auth needed for parametric builders.
context_for_next_testing_agent: For chest hinge testing: bboxes render inside `[data-testid="chest-bboxes"]` as `Name\n{X}×{Y}×{Z} mm` blocks (parse with regex `([A-Za-z ]+?)\s*\n\s*([\d.]+)×([\d.]+)×([\d.]+)\s*mm`). The topHingedBox checkbox is at `[data-testid="chest-hingedtop"] input[type="checkbox"]` (needs .check(force=True)). Kickstand at `[data-testid="chest-lidkickstand"] input[type="checkbox"]`. Add-to-workspace at `[data-testid="chest-add-workspace"]`. Default chest at hingedtop-only produces Frame 80×65.4×102.4 mm (vs iter-151.23 baseline 80×65.4×101.2 — the +1.2 mm Z is the iter-151.28 hinge-lift). Hinged-lid part is 80×69.3×5.4 mm. With kickstand at default 100°: 80×66.914×104.864 (matches iter-151.23 kickstand growth pattern). For box hinge testing: dialog testid `[data-testid="box-designer-dialog"]`, lid mode select `[data-testid="box-lid-mode"]` with values `none | drop | friction | sliding | hinged` — use page.select_option('...', value='hinged'). Bboxes at `[data-testid="box-designer-bboxes"]`. Add-to-workspace at `[data-testid="box-designer-add-workspace"]`. Default box (60×40×30, lidThickness=2, wall=2, floor=2) in hinged mode: Box body 60×45.2×33.2 mm, Lid 60×45.2×5.2 mm. There is no 'chest-2 test' compartment testid or explicit hinge-mode radio — the lid mode is a single <select>. Workspace 'What's New' popup will overlap dialog footers; use force=True when clicking add-to-workspace.

detailed_results.T1_chest_hinged_lid_Z_bbox: PASS — Hinged lid Z = 5.4 mm at default params (hingeLidThickness=3, knuckleR=2.7). Matches spec `= max(hingeLidThickness, 2*knuckleR) = 2*knuckleR`. Previously (before iter-151.28) would have been 3 mm.
detailed_results.T2_chest_frame_Z_bbox: PASS — Frame Z = 102.4 mm at hingedtop-only (vs iter-151.23 baseline 101.2). Delta +1.2 mm = knuckleR − hingeLidThickness/2 = 2.7 − 1.5. Confirms knuckle base raised by knuckleR above frame top plane.
detailed_results.T3_box_hinged_lid_Z_bbox: PASS — Lid Z = 5.2 mm at default (lidThickness=2, knuckleR=2.6). Matches spec `bodyH + lidThickness + knuckleR` for world-Z max, i.e. local-Z extent of 2*knuckleR = 5.2.
detailed_results.T4_box_body_Z_bbox: PASS — Box body Z = 33.2 mm (H=30, bodyH=H−lidThickness=28, knuckle top = bodyH + 2*knuckleR = 28 + 5.2 = 33.2). Matches spec `bodyH + knuckleR` for axis with knuckleR extra for knuckle top.
detailed_results.T5_chest_hinged_off_regression: PASS — Toggling topHingedBox OFF restores exactly Frame 80×60×96 and Top cap 86×66×4 (matches pre-fix baseline). Zero delta on X/Y/Z. Only the hinged-box code path was changed.
detailed_results.T6_box_lidmode_none_regression: PASS — Setting lidMode='none' restores Box body 60×40×30 (bodyH = H when no lid). Lid part correctly disappears from bboxes.
detailed_results.T7_kickstand_regression: PASS — With kickstand ON at default angle=100°, Frame = 80×66.914×104.864 (vs hinged-only 80×65.4×102.4). Growth ΔY +1.514, ΔZ +2.464 consistent with iter-151.23 kickstand stop-bar tilt (β = 100 + 2·halfAngDeg). Stop bar still exists and interferes at ~100°.
detailed_results.T8_chest_add_to_workspace: PASS — Adds 4 parts (Frame, Drawer 1, Drawer 2, Hinged lid) to workspace with correct bboxes. Assembled view shows knuckles as small cylindrical bumps on back-top edge — matches the intentional piano-hinge detail described in the fix RCA.
detailed_results.T9_box_add_to_workspace: PASS — Adds 2 parts (Box body, Lid) to workspace. Screenshot shows visible knuckles on box back-top edge and matching interlocking knuckles on the lid. No black gaps, no broken geometry, axle-hole carve appears intact.
detailed_results.T10_visual_no_regressions: PASS — Both preview panels (dialog live view) and workspace show clean rounded slabs + welded knuckles + rib + drilled axle hole. No manifold failures, no z-fighting artifacts visible.
